description_long: This object is a compact, retro-style record player with a rectangular main body and a large, circular black platter extending from one side. The body is primarily yellow with black accents and features a transparent, slightly domed cover over the platter area, revealing some of the internal mechanisms. The platter appears to be made of black plastic or vinyl and is proportionally large compared to the main housing. A slender tonearm is visible, positioned to the side of the platter, and there are several control buttons and switches on the top surface, along with printed labels. The device has metallic fasteners and feet, suggesting stability and portability, and the overall design is reminiscent of portable record players from the late 20th century. The finish is a mix of matte and glossy surfaces, with some reflective elements on the transparent cover.
description: A compact, retro yellow and black record player with a large black platter, transparent cover, and visible tonearm and controls. The design is portable and features both matte and glossy finishes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the black platter, the transparent cover, and the yellow and black body with visible control panel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rectangular face of the player, the circular platter extending behind, and the control panel on top.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the platter, transparent cover, and the tonearm resting to the side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the player, the platter, and the tonearm extending outward.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and prominently visible, occupying a large circular area relative to the rectangular body. It is black, likely made of plastic or vinyl, with a smooth, slightly glossy finish and subtle grooves typical of record platters. The platter extends beyond the main housing, making it a dominant feature of the device.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, slender, and metallic, positioned to the side of the platter. It appears to be of medium length, with a joint near the base for pivoting. The tonearm is resting near the edge of the platter, ready for use.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible as a small component at the end of the tonearm, appearing as a fine, pointed element. It is attached to the tonearm and seems to be a separate, delicate part, likely made of metal and plastic.
attribute_control_knobs: Several control buttons and switches are visible on the top surface of the record player. They are rectangular and circular, made of plastic, and colored black and yellow to match the device. Some have printed labels or markings indicating their function.
attribute_dust_cover: A transparent dust cover is present, made of clear plastic or glass, with a slightly domed shape. It covers the platter area and is attached to the main body, likely hinged at the back. The cover is glossy and allows visibility of the platter and tonearm beneath.
